options.summary: Get list of task IDs executed on the blueprint with given ID.
options.description: None

get.summary: Get list of tasks executed on blueprint with given ID.
get.description: None

get.summary: Get details of a given task executed on blueprint with given ID.
get.description: None

put.summary: Update an ARCA instance for a blueprint
put.description:
Updates an ARCA instance with the given {arca_id} in the blueprint
with the given {blueprint_id}.

delete.summary: Delete an ARCA instance for a blueprint
delete.description:
Deletes an ARCA instance with the given {arca_id} in the blueprint
with the given {blueprint_id}.

get.summary: Get ARCA instance for the given blueprint.
get.description:
Get configureed ARCA instance with given {arca_id} for blueprint
with given {blueprint_id}.

post.summary: Create an ARCA instance for given blueprint.
post.description:
Creates an ARCA instance with given system fault model for the blueprint
with specified {blueprint_id}. Returns an object with "id" key which is
the ID of the created ARCA instance. This ID can be used to GET the
status fo the ARCA instance.

get.summary: Get list of ARCA instances for the given blueprint.
get.description: Get all configured ARCA instances for blueprint by {blueprint_id}.

put.summary: Update predefined probe through simplified set of parameters.
put.description: None

get.summary: Get parameters of a probe specific to the predefined probe name
get.description: None

post.summary: Instantiate a predefined probe with parameters.
post.description: None

get.summary: List all the predefined probes according to the blueprint design.
get.description: None

put.summary: Update a dashboard with the given dashboard ID.
put.description: Returns an object with id of the updated dashboard.

get.summary: Get dashboard details by its ID.
get.description:
Returns details of the dashboard by the specified {blueprint_id}
and {dashboard_id}.

delete.summary: Delete a dashboard with a given ID.
delete.description:
Delete the given dashboard by the specified {blueprint_id} and
{dashboard_id}.

post.summary: Create a dashboard for the given blueprint.
post.description: Returns an object with id of the new dashboard.

get.summary: Get dashboards for the given blueprint.
get.description:
Get all dashboards for the given blueprint by
the specified {blueprint_id}.

put.summary: Update a widget with the given widget ID.
put.description:
If a widget doesn't exist, then create it and return its id.
Otherwise, update existing widget.

delete.summary: Delete a widget with a given ID.
delete.description:
Delete the given widget by the specified {blueprint_id} and
{widget_id}.

get.summary: Get widget details by its ID.
get.description:
Returns details of the widget by the specified {blueprint_id}
and {widget_id}.

For the given heatmap specified by {blueprint_id} and
{widget_id}, report table data, i.e. number of matching
items and number of their anomalies for each cell specified
by a column tag and a row tag.

post.summary: Create a widget for the given blueprint.
post.description: Returns an object with id of the new widget.

get.summary: Get widgets for the given blueprint.
get.description:
Get all widgets for the given blueprint by
the specified {blueprint_id}.

post.description: Create dashboard with the parameters from the payload.

patch.summary: Update predefined dashbord.
patch.description: The only parameter that could be changed is 'disabled'.

get.summary: Get predefined dashboards for the given blueprint.

Since some processors are able to auto-enable telemetry
services, there might be conflicts/misconfigurations.

put.description:
Completely replace a probe with a new one keeping the id.
Resources used by the old probe will be freed and new probe
will be configured.

patch.summary: Patch a probe for the given blueprint.
patch.description:
Allows changing meta-data fields like label, description, tags,
and stage information such as description, units, and tags.

get.summary: Get a probe for the given blueprint.
get.description:
Returns details of the probe by the specified {blueprint_id} and
{probe_id}.

delete.description:
Delete the given probe by the specified {blueprint_id} and
{probe_id}.

get.summary: Get a list of messages for the given probe.
get.description:
Returns a list of probe messages by the specified {blueprint_id} and
{probe_id}.

post.summary: Get a status of the given stage in the probe.
post.description:
Returns a status of a stage including created series and their
samples.

post.summary: Create a probe for the given blueprint.

the specified {blueprint_id}. Returns an object with the id key which
is a name of the created probe.

get.summary: Get all anomalies for a given blueprint
get.description:
Retrieves all anomalies for the blueprint specified by {blueprint_id}.
Default limit on return is 10,000 entries.

post.summary: Execute the specified query and output query trace information
post.description:
It returns all query search steps and results found after each step.
Used in Graph Debugger UI.

get.summary: Get status of configuration deployment for specified blueprint
get.description:
Get status of configuration deployment for blueprint and all associated systems
specified by {blueprint_id}",

get.summary: Get per-node anomalies count for a given blueprint
get.description:
Retrieves a count of anomalies per each node in blueprint with id
{blueprint_id}

post.summary:
This API permits validating configuration templates prior to committing
            them to the graph by exercising them against specific systems within
            the blueprint. This helps to avoid committing templates to the blueprint
            whic
post.description: This endpoint is only valid for the 'freeform' reference design.

post.summary: Roll back to a previous revision of blueprint
post.description: None

get.summary: Retrieve diff between staged and deployed blueprint
get.description:
Retrieve diff between staged and deployed blueprint.
Three different modes are supported with varying amount of details:
* 'digest' - only counts how many nodes or links changed
* 'lite' - includes digest and logical diff, but only ids and labels of changed elements
* 'full' - digest and full logical diff

post.summary: Execute the GraphQL query in readonly mode
post.description:
Execute the GraphQL query on the blueprint instance {blueprint_id}.
If the query tries to change the blueprint, an error will be returned.

get.description:
Returns a list of available processor types for a particular blueprint
with the given {blueprint_id}.

Retrieves the number of anomalies per each 2-tuple of role and anomaly_type
in the blueprint specified by {blueprint_id}.

options.description:
Each reference design defines Blueprint API extensions which are
bridged by this method. See your reference design documentation for
specific APIs.

           deployed/operation blueprint.

merged with the system agent profile to produce an effective config
of the system agent.

authenticate to the device. Prior to this call, the system-agent requires
either a username and password or profile during the POST
to /api/system-agents or PATCH/PUT to /api/system-agents/{agent_id}.

config of the system agent is a combination of the user config and the
system agent profile referenced by the user config, if any.

delete.summary:
Delete the package(s) specified by package name. The name can be
       a 'packagename==someversion' to delete a specific version of a
       specific package or just 'packagename' to delete all versions of
       the specified package.

get.summary: Return list of all versions of a package
get.description: None

post.summary:
Add a new package to AOS package manager. The binary contents of the
       package should be the body of this request.
       package type is by filename extension, can be .egg .whl or .gz.
       package name must follow python package name convention,
post.description: None

get.description: Response: 200

post.description:

The change only affects the new probe scheduling, it will not
affect existing IBA units.

post.summary: Create devices in a batch operation.

Create a set of new systems in a batch according to the specified
attributes. This operation is not atomic, so it creates only
possible devices.

Retrieives all anomalies.
Default limit on return is 10,000 entries.
Response: 200

post.summary: Create a clone of existing Device Profile and optionally any related Interface Maps
post.description: Create a clone of existing Device Profile with the specified id with required edits to any section except 'ports'. If any Interface Map referenced by original Device Profile is also supplied, it will be cloned and updated to reflect newly cloned Device Profile. The 'created_at' and 'last_modified_at' fields are ignored.  If no 'id' is supplied, one will be generated.

get.description: Get the most recent events, with a maximum specified by 'max'

post.summary: Update devices in a batch operation.
post.description:
Update user configuration attributes, such as location, HCL-model
and admin state, of a set of systems in a batch operation.

get.description:
This effectively returns an entry per each type of alert that is sent via
the protobuf streaming mechanism.  Each entry is a string meant for human
consumption that described the given alert.
